title: User plane relocation for multicast sessions in a centralized unit
abstract: Techniques for relocating at least one multicast (MC) session from a first CU-UP (CU-UP1) to a second CU-UP (CU-UP2) are described In operation, an MC Bearer Context Setup Request for relocating the at least one MC session is received, where the at least one MC session exists between the CU-UP1 and one or more DUs. The Bearer Context Setup Request comprises a first list of Transport Network Layer Addresses (TNLAs) associated with the one or more DUs. In response to the MC Bearer Context Setup Request, an MC Bearer Context Setup Response comprising a second list of CU TNLAs is received, where the second list of CU TNLAs is associated with the CU-UP2, and second list of CU TNLAs corresponds to the first list of TNLAs.
claims:
Claims We Claim: 1. A method implemented by a control plane (CU-CP) of a centralized unit, the method comprising: transmitting, to a second user plane (CU-UP2) of the centralized unit, a multicast (MC) Bearer Context Setup Request for relocating at least one MC session from a first user plane (CU-UP1 ) of the centralized unit to the CU-UP2, the at least one MC session existing between the CU-UP1 and one or more distributed units (DU), and the Bearer Context Setup Request comprising a first list of Transport Network Layer Addresses (TNLAs), wherein each TNLA from amongst the first list of TNLAs is associated with a DU from amongst the one or more DUs; receiving, from the CU-UP2, an MC Bearer Context Setup Response in response to the MC Bearer Context Setup Request, the MC Bearer Context Setup Response comprising a second list of CU TNLAs, wherein each CU TNLA from amongst the second list of CU TNLAs is associated with the CU-UP2, and each CU TNLA corresponds to a TNLA included in the first list of TNLAs; and transmitting, to the one or more DUs, the second list of CU TNLAs for relocating the at least one MC session to the CU-UP2. 2. A method implemented by a control plane (CU-CP) of a centralized unit, the method comprising: transmitting, to a second user plane (CU-UP2) of the centralized unit, a multicast (MC) Bearer Context Setup Request for relocating at least one MC session from a first user plane (CU-UP1 ) of the centralized unit to the CU-UP2, the at least one MC session existing between the CU-UP1 and one or more distributed units (DU); transmitting, to the CU-UP2, an MC Bearer Context Modification Request comprising a first list of Transport Network Layer Addresses (TNLAs), wherein each TNLA from amongst the first list of TNLAs is associated with a DU from amongst the one or more DUs; and 

 receiving, from the CU-UP2, an MC Bearer Context Modification Response in response to the MC Bearer Context Modification Request, the MC Bearer Context Modification Response including a second list of CU TNLAs, wherein each CU TNLA from amongst the second list of CU TNLAs is associated with the CU-UP2, and each CU TNLA corresponds to a TNLA included in the first list of TNLAs; and transmitting, to the one or more DUs, the second list of CU TNLAs for relocating the at least one MC session to the CU-UP2. 3. The method as claimed in claim 1 or 2, wherein the MC Bearer Context Setup Request is transmitted on receiving a session relocation indication for relocating the at least one MC session. 4. The method as claimed in claim 1 or 2, further comprising: receiving a load indicator from the CU-UP1 , the load indicator being indicative of MC resource utilization of one or more MC sessions existing between the CU-UP1 and a plurality of Dus, the one or more MC sessions including the at least one MC session; and triggering transmission of the MC Bearer Context Setup Request to the CU-UP 2 for relocating the at least one MC session on determining the MC resource utilization to be beyond a threshold. 5. The method as claimed in claim 1 or 2, further comprising: transmitting, to the CU-UP1 , an MC Bearer Context Modification Request comprising the second list of CU TNLAs associated with the CU-UP2; and receiving, from the CU-UP1 , an MC Bearer Context Modification Response in response to the MC Bearer Context Modification Request. 6. The method as claimed in any of claims 1 to 5, further comprising: 

 transmitting, to a 5 th Generation Core (5GC), an NG application protocol MC Distribution Modification Request comprising the second list of CU TNLAs. 7. A method implemented by a first user plane (CU-UP1 ) of a centralized unit, the method comprising: monitoring MC resource utilisation associated with one or more MC sessions existing between the CU-UP1 and a plurality of distributed units (Dlls); and transmitting a session relocation indication for relocating at least one MC session from the one or more sessions on determining the MC resource utilization to be beyond a threshold. 8. A method implemented by a second user plane (CU-UP2) of a centralized unit, the method comprising: receiving, from a control plane (CU-CP) of the centralized unit, a multicast (MC) Bearer Context Setup Request for relocating at least one MC session from a first user plane (CU-UP1 ) of the centralized unit to the CU-UP2, the at least one MC session existing between the CU-UP1 and one or more distributed units (Dlls), and the MC Bearer Context Setup Request comprising a first list of Transport Network Layer Addresses (TNLAs), wherein each TNLA from amongst the first list of TNLAs is associated with a DU from amongst the one or more DUs; generating a second list of CU TNLAs, each CU TNLA from amongst the second list of CU TNLAs being associated with the CU-UP2, each CU TNLA corresponds to a TNLA included in the first list of TNLAs, and the second list of CU TNLAs being used for relocating the at least one MC session to the CU-UP2; and transmitting, to the CU-CP, an MC Bearer Context Setup Response comprising the second list of CU TNLAs in response to the Bearer Context Setup Request. 9. A method implemented by a second user plane (CU-UP2) of a centralized unit, the method comprising: receiving, from a control plane (CU-CP) of the centralized unit, a multicast (MC) Bearer Context Setup Request for relocating at least one MC session from a first user plane (CU-UP1 ) of the centralized unit to the CU-UP2, the at least one MC session existing between the CU-UP1 and one or more distributed units (DUs); receiving, from the CU-CP, an MC Bearer Context Modification Request comprising a first list of Transport Network Layer Addresses (TNLAs), wherein each TNLA from amongst the first list of TNLAs is associated with a DU from amongst the one or more DUs; generating a second list of CU TNLAs, each CU TNLA from amongst the second list of CU TNLAs being associated with the CU-UP2, each CU TNLA corresponds to a TNLA included in the first list of TNLAs, and the second list of CU TNLAs being used for relocating the at least one MC session to the CU-UP2; and transmitting, to the CU-CP, an MC Bearer Context Modification Response comprising the second list of CU TNLAs in response to the Bearer Context Modification Request. 10. A method implemented by a distributed unit (DU), the method comprising: receiving a multicast (MC) Distribution Modification Request for relocating at least one MC session from a first user plane (CU-UP1 ) to a second user plane (CU-UP2) of a centralized unit, the at least one MC session existing between the CU-UP1 and the DU, the MC Distribution Modification Request comprising first list of Transport Network Layer Addresses (TNLAs) comprising one of a TNLA associated with the DU and a CU TNLA associated with the CU-UP1 , and a second list of CU TNLAs associated with the CU-UP2, the second list of CU TNLAs comprising a CU TNLA corresponding to a TNLA from amongst the first 

list of TNLAs, and the second list of CU TNLAs being usable for relocating the at least one MC session to the CU-UP2; and relocating the at least one MC session to the CU-UP2 based on the second list of CU TNLAs. 11 . A control plane (CU-CP) of a centralized unit, the CU-CP comprising: at least one CU-CP processor; a computer-readable medium comprising instructions that, when executed by the at least one CU-CP processor, causes the CU-CP to: transmit, to a second user plane (CU-UP2) of the centralized unit, a multicast (MC) Bearer Context Setup Request for relocating at least one MC session from a first user plane (CU-UP1 ) of the centralized unit to the CU-UP2, the at least one MC session existing between the CU-UP1 and one or more distributed units (DU), and the Bearer Context Setup Request comprising a first list of Transport Network Layer Addresses (TNLAs), wherein each TNLA from amongst the first list of TNLAs is associated with a DU from amongst the one or more DUs; and receive, from the CU-UP2, an MC Bearer Context Setup Response in response to the MC Bearer Context Setup Request, the MC Bearer Context Setup Response comprising a second list of CU TNLAs, wherein each CU TNLA from amongst the second list of CU TNLAs is associated with the CU-UP2, and each CU TNLA corresponds to a TNLA included in the first list of TNLAs; and transmit, to the one or more DUs, the second list of CU TNLAs for relocating the at least one MC session to the CU-UP2. 12. A control plane (CU-CP) of a centralized unit, the CU-CP comprising: at least one CU-CP processor; a computer-readable medium comprising instructions that, when executed by the at least one CU-CP processor, causes the CU-CP to: 

 transmit, to a second user plane (CU-UP2) of the centralized unit, a multicast (MC) Bearer Context Setup Request for relocating at least one MC session from a first user plane (CU-UP1 ) of the centralized unit to the CU-UP2, the at least one MC session existing between the CU-UP1 and one or more distributed units (DU), and transmit, to the CU-UP2, an MC Bearer Context Modification Request comprising a first list of Transport Network Layer Addresses (TNLAs), wherein each TNLA from amongst the first list of TNLAs is associated with a DU from amongst the one or more DUs; and receive, from the CU-UP2, an MC Bearer Context Modification Response in response to the MC Bearer Context Modification Request, the MC Bearer Context Modification Response including a second list of CU TNLAs, wherein each CU TNLA from amongst the second list of CU TNLAs is associated with the CU-UP2, and each CU TNLA corresponds to a TNLA included in the first list of TNLAs; and transmit, to the one or more DUs, the second list of CU TNLAs for relocating the at least one MC session to the CU-UP2. 13. The CU-CP as claimed in claim 11 or 12, wherein the at least one CU-CP processor is to cause the CU-CP to transmit the MC Bearer Context Setup Request on receiving a session relocation indication for relocating of the at least one MC session. 14. The CU-CP as claimed in claim 11 or 12, wherein the at least one CU-CP processor is to cause the CU-CP to: receive a load indicator from the CU-UP1 , the load indicator being indicative of a multicast resource utilization of one or more MC sessions existing between the CU-UP1 and a plurality of DUs, the one or more sessions including the at least one MC session; and 

 trigger transmission of the Bearer Context Setup Request to the CU UP 2 for relocating the at least one MC session on determining the multicast resource utilization to be beyond a threshold. 15. The CU-CP as claimed in any claims 11 to 14, wherein the at least one CU-CP processor causes the CU-CP to further: transmit, to the CU-UP1 , an MC Bearer Context Modification Request comprising the second list of CU TNLAs associated with the CU-UP2; and receive, from the CU-UP1 , an MC Bearer Context Modification Response in response to the MC Bearer Context Modification Request. 16. The CU-CP as claimed in any claims 11 to 15, wherein the at least one CU-CP processor causes the CU-CP to transmit, to a 5 th Generation Core (5GC), an NG application protocol MC Distribution Modification Request comprising the second list of CU TNLAs. 17. A first user plane (CU-UP1 ) of a centralized unit, the CU-UP1 comprising: at least one CU-UP1 processor; a computer-readable medium comprising instructions that, when executed by the at least one CU-UP1 processor, causes the CU-UP1 to: monitor MC resource utilisation associated with one or more MC sessions existing between the CU-UP1 and a plurality of distributed units (DUs); and transmit a session relocation indication for relocating at least one MC session from the one or more sessions on determining the MC resource utilization to be beyond a threshold. 18. A second user plane (CU-UP2) of a centralized unit, the CU-UP2 comprising: at least one CU-UP2 processor; 

 a computer-readable medium comprising instructions that, when executed by the at least one CU-UP2 processor, causes the CU-UP2 to: receive, from a control plane (CU-CP) of the centralized unit, a MC Bearer Context Setup Request for relocating at least one MC session from a first user plane (CU-UP1 ) of the centralized unit to the CU-UP2, the at least one MC session existing between the CU-UP1 and one or more distributed units (Dlls), and the MC Bearer Context Setup Request comprising a first list of Transport Network Layer Addresses (TNLAs), wherein each TNLA from amongst the first list of TNLAs is associated with a DU from amongst the one or more DUs; generate a second list of CU TNLAs, each CU TNLA from amongst the second list of CU TNLAs being associated with the CU-UP2, each CU TNLA corresponds to a TNLA included in the first list of TNLAs, and the second list of CU TNLAs being used for relocating the at least one MC session to the CU-UP2; and transmit, to the CU-CP, an MC Bearer Context Setup Response comprising the second list of CU TNLAs in response to the Bearer Context Setup Request. 19. A second user plane (CU-UP2) of a centralized unit, the CU-UP2 comprising: at least one CU-UP2 processor; a computer-readable medium comprising instructions that, when executed by the at least one CU-UP2 processor, causes the CU-UP2 to: receive, from a control plane (CU-CP) of the centralized unit, a multicast (MC) Bearer Context Setup Request for relocating at least one MC session from a first user plane (CU-UP1 ) of the centralized unit to the CU-UP2, the at least one MC session existing between the CU-UP1 and one or more distributed units (DUs); 

 receive, from the CU-CP, an MC Bearer Context Modification Request comprising a first list of Transport Network Layer Addresses (TNLAs), wherein each TNLA from amongst the first list of TNLAs is associated with a DU from amongst the one or more DUs; generate a second list of CU TNLAs, each CU TNLA from amongst the second list of CU TNLAs being associated with the CU-UP2, each CU TNLA corresponds to a TNLA included in the first list of TNLAs, and the second list of CU TNLAs being used for relocating the at least one MC session to the CU-UP2; and transmit, to the CU-CP, an MC Bearer Context Modification Response comprising the second list of CU TNLAs in response to the Bearer Context Modification Request. 20. A distributed unit (DU) comprising: at least one DU processor; a computer-readable medium comprising instructions that, when executed by the at least one DU processor, causes the DU to: receive an MC Distribution Modification Request for relocating at least one MC session from a first user plane (CU-UP1 ) to a second user plane (CU-UP2) of a centralized unit, the at least one MC session existing between the CU-UP1 and the DU, the MC Distribution Modification Request comprising a first list of Transport Network Layer Addresses (TNLAs) comprising a DU TNLA associated with the DU or a CU TNLA associated with the CU-UP1 , and a second list of CU TNLAs associated with the CU-UP2, the second list of CU TNLAs comprising a CU TNLA corresponding to a TNLA from amongst the first list of TNLAs, and the second list of CU TNLAs being usable for relocating the at least one MC session to the CU-UP2; and relocate the at least one MC session to the CU-UP2 based on the second list of CU TNLAs.
